content: General hobby-simulation-project pattern (game-dev community consensus, cf. 'walking skeleton' / vertical-slice advice): projects that pursue realism/fidelity (physics accuracy, visual detail, expanded scope) before a minimal end-to-end loop is playable tend to stall or never ship, because early investment in subsystems (e.g. detailed performance models, multiple procedures) built before the core loop is validated is frequently reworked once real usage reveals what the loop actually needs.
